title: Open Problems PBMC Single-Cell Perturbation DGE Task

data_urls.description: Open Problems single-cell perturbation competition page
data_urls.access_notes: Kaggle account, accepted competition rules, or mirrored public assets may be required.

data_urls.description: Open Problems perturbation prediction task repository
data_urls.access_notes: Use repository loaders and metadata to verify exact file names and schema.

instructions: Use the official competition page or task repository to identify current data access instructions.
For hard blinded capsules, expose compound names or IDs but withhold SMILES from the vanilla-visible bundle.
Recover chemical structures, targets, pathways, and cell-state metadata as external resources during SPECTRA.

recommended_axes.name: chemical_structure_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Morgan, ChemBERTa, or other compound similarity after external structure recovery.

recommended_axes.name: target_pathway_overlap
recommended_axes.definition: Drug target, pathway, LINCS, or mechanism-of-action relatedness.

recommended_axes.name: cell_state_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Cell-type ontology, baseline expression, or multiome-state similarity.

useful_for: Hard agent-discovery tests where schema-obvious axes are insufficient.
Testing whether SPECTRA agents retrieve external chemical and pathway structure.
leakage_risks: Visible SMILES makes chemical novelty too obvious for agent-discovery tests.
Public leaderboard/test labels can contaminate evaluation.
Drug and cell-type names may encode support if split construction is not blinded.
